summary: Pleasant audience recording captures a tight spring‑87 performance highlighted by stellar Black Peter and The Other One.
review: The show is praised for its crisp, power‑packed playing that epitomizes the joyful energy of Spring ’87, with Black Peter and The Other One singled out as standout moments. Reviewers note an accurate and pleasant audience source that delivers more bass and tighter mids than typical recordings, making it one of the better tapes from this night.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both performance (tight, vibrant, no sour notes) and recording quality (pleasant AUD with good bass/mids), yielding a weighted average around 4.3.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Accurate and pleasant audience capture; reviewers highlight more bass, tighter mids, and overall clarity that may be better than other tapes from the same night.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight, crisp, and power‑packed throughout; no sour notes or energy dips, delivering a vibrant spring ’87 vibe.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jerry's guitar work is praised as 'so well' and a key part of the joyous performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil is heard saying "Billy and Mick" early in the first set; his presence adds to the tightness of the show.
